rows.v: Contact Center & Back Office Services RFP
Ubiquity is not a subsidiary and operates as an independent, privately held company.

Ubiquity will provide copies of its most recent audited financial statements for each of the last three completed fiscal years through a secure email address provided by the customer, and will share the information and documents using secured email for compliance purposes.

Ubiquity supports phone as a primary channel and can also support omnichannel service, including in-app, email, and chat.
It integrates with client programs as an extension of the client’s team, using existing tools while addressing security issues prior to launch and aligning on required tools, websites, and workflows .
If a client does not have a telephony system, Ubiquity provides an AVAYA-based infrastructure that supports inbound and outbound communications, hosts its own IVRs, and can create complex call flows for caller authentication and self-service .
It also supports integration with major softphone platforms, including Twilio Flex, Zendesk, Genesys, TalkDesk, and Amazon Connect, and can accommodate client-hosted or managed telephony platforms .
The inTouch® system provides agents with visibility into the caller’s IVR path and supports real-time monitoring, reporting, and analytics, including call recording, agent availability tracking, and secure access controls.

568 Broadway, #601 New York, NY 10012.
Ubiquity is headquartered in New York, NY.
1) Enter your responses in the 'Questionnaire' sheet.
2) If a question is not applicable, enter 'N/A' and briefly explain why.
3) When attachments are requested, provide a URL/link or file name in the Attachment(s)/URL(s) column.
4) For 'Yes/No + Narrative' items, select Yes/No/N/A in the Y/N/N/A column and add supporting detail in the Narrative column.
5) If you need more space, add rows below the question and repeat the Question ID.
Structured table (paste if small; otherwise attach)
Answer each question. Yellow cells are for vendor input.
Provide a brief overview of your company, including ownership structure, years in operation, and primary service offerings.

Ubiquity is a privately held business process outsourcing (BPO) provider founded in 2012, with 14 years of operational experience. The company specializes in customer experience (CX), back-office operations, and risk & compliance services, supporting global clients across multiple industries.

Primary service offerings include:

• Customer Experience (CX): Omnichannel support (voice, chat, email, SMS), technical support, sales, retention, and collections.
• Risk & Compliance: Identity verification, fraud prevention, chargeback management, and regulatory support.
• Back-Office Services: Data processing, reporting, analytics, and operational support.
• Consulting & Optimization: Workforce management, QA design, process improvement, and digital transformation.
• Global Delivery: 24/7/365 operations across onshore, nearshore, and offshore sites with multilingual capabilities.

Ubiquity delivers flexible engagement models and scalable operations supported by technology, analytics, and experienced leadership teams.
Provide your financial stability overview (e.g., most recent annual revenue range, profitability trend) and confirm ability to support multi-year engagement.

Ubiquity is financially stable and supports long-term, multi-year client engagements across global operations.

While detailed financial statements are shared under NDA during contracting, Ubiquity maintains consistent revenue growth, strong client retention, and profitability across its portfolio.

The company has demonstrated the ability to scale large programs (e.g., ramping 300 to 1,000 FTEs) and invest in infrastructure, technology, and talent to support enterprise clients over multi-year engagements.

Describe your experience supporting U.S.-based consumer financial services (e.g., lending, servicing, collections). Include years of experience and client examples.

Ubiquity has over a decade of experience (since 2012) supporting U.S.-based consumer financial services clients, including fintechs, digital lenders, and credit providers.

Lending & Servicing
• Supports $50B+ in managed loan portfolios  
• Processes 2M+ monthly interactions  
• Services include onboarding, verification, servicing, and portfolio transfers  

Collections
• Supporting collections since 2015  
• Achieved up to 15% improvement in collection rates  
• Compliance-first approach aligned with FDCPA  

Card & Account Services
• End-to-end credit card servicing (activation, disputes, payments, rewards)  

Ubiquity combines operational scale with regulatory compliance and customer-centric delivery.
Provide at least three relevant client references for comparable scope (voice + back office and/or staff augmentation).
Describe your proposed operating model for this engagement (staff augmentation vs managed services vs hybrid).

Ubiquity supports flexible operating models, including staff augmentation, managed services, and hybrid delivery.

For this engagement, Ubiquity proposes a staffing model comprised of local and gloabl support combining:
• Dedicated teams aligned to client processes and KPIs  
• Embedded leadership and QA oversight  
• Scalable staffing across global delivery sites  

This approach enables operational control, flexibility, and cost efficiency while maintaining consistent service quality.

Delivery can be fully onshore, offshore, or blended based on client requirements.
Confirm ability to support the hours of operation (Mon–Fri 7:00am–11:00pm CT; Sat 7:00am–5:00pm CT) and describe any constraints.

Ubiquity can fully support the required hours of operation:

• Monday–Friday: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM CT  
• Saturday: 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM CT  

All delivery centers operate 24/7/365, allowing flexible scheduling and full leadership coverage aligned to program hours.

No operational constraints are anticipated.
Describe your approach to scaling up/down (seasonality, campaigns, volume spikes) and minimum notice periods required.

Ubiquity manages scaling through forecasting, capacity planning, and flexible staffing models.

Scaling Up
• Workforce planning aligned to 12–24 week forecasts  
• Proven ramp: 0–100 FTEs in 90 days; 300–1,000 in 150 days  
• Multi-site recruiting and cross-trained talent pools  

Scaling Down
• Reallocation of staff across programs to minimize attrition  
• Compliance with local labor laws  

Real Time Flexing
• Intraday management with 15–30 minute interval adjustments  
• Cross-skilled agents deployed based on demand  

Standard Notice Period
• Standard: 60–90 days for large-scale ramp planning
Describe how you would structure teams across workgroups (Collections, Customer Service, Chat, Back Office) and how cross-training would be managed.

Ubiquity uses a layered team structure to support delivery across workstreams (Collections, Customer Service, Chat, Back Office). We have provided our standards below, but we will build a team and support structure that addresses Santander's specific business needs:

Standard Leadership and Support Ratios
• Team Lead: 1:15  
• QA: 1:50  
• Trainer: 1:75  
• Operations Manager: 1:100  

Additional roles include Workforce Management, SMEs, HR, IT, and Client Success.

Cross-Training Approach
• Structured onboarding (3–4 weeks) + nesting (2 weeks)  
• Progressive skill development from simple to complex tasks  
• Ongoing refreshers, QA-driven coaching, and certification gates  
• Multi-skill training enables flexibility across channels (voice/chat/back office)

This model ensures scalability, quality control, and workforce flexibility.
Describe your recruiting model, screening process, and typical time-to-fill for agent and support roles in proposed locations.

Ubiquity uses a multi-channel recruiting strategy across all delivery locations, including referrals (40%+ of hires), job platforms, social media, hiring events, and agency partnerships. As part of our recruitment process, we will work to build the right profile to fit Santander's needs. This us our default recruitment set up:

Screening Process
• English proficiency (CEFR B2–C1)  
• Role-based assessments (typing, systems, problem-solving)  
• Structured HR and Operations interviews  
• Background checks (criminal, employment, education, OFAC)  

Profile
• Minimum 6 months contact center experience (80%+ have 2+ years)

Time-to-Fill
• 20–30 agents within ~4 weeks (standard roles)

Recruiting is aligned with client-specific profiles and ramp timelines.
Provide your attrition management approach and historical annual attrition range for similar programs (by site if possible).

Ubiquity maintains low attrition rates supported by strong employee engagement and career development programs.

Global Attrition (Recent)
• ~4.75% – 5.5% annually  

By Site (YTD Example)
• Bacolod: 4.38%  
• San Salvador: 6.77%  
• Mission, TX: 10.5%  

Retention Strategy
• Career progression and internal promotions  
• Incentives and recognition programs  
• Flexible scheduling and wellness initiatives  

Attrition is monitored using a 12-month rolling methodology and managed proactively through engagement and workforce planning.
Describe your approach to background checks, employment eligibility verification, and ongoing compliance.

Ubiquity conducts comprehensive pre-employment and ongoing compliance checks to ensure workforce integrity and regulatory adherence.

**Pre-Employment Screening**
• Criminal background checks (e.g., NBI clearance in the Philippines)  
• Employment and education verification  
• OFAC sanctions screening  

**Ongoing Compliance**
• Annual compliance and security recertification  
• Quarterly internal audits and annual external audits  
• Alignment with PCI DSS Level 1, SOC 2 Type II, and ISO 27001  

Audit findings are tracked through a centralized action registry with defined remediation plans and timelines.
Confirm ability to meet or exceed the minimum leadership/QA coverage ratios (TL 1:30; Sup 1:15–1:18; Mgr 1:75; QA 1:75; Site Director per site). If proposing alternatives, provide rationale.

Ubiquity meets or exceeds required leadership and QA coverage ratios:

• Team Lead: 1:15 (exceeds 1:30 requirement)  
• Supervisor: 1:15 (meets requirement)  
• QA: 1:50 (exceeds 1:75 requirement)  
• Manager: 1:100 (flexible based on program needs)  

Ratios can be adjusted based on complexity, regulatory requirements, and client preferences.
Describe your workforce management (WFM) capabilities (forecasting, scheduling, intraday management) and tools used.

Ubiquity provides end-to-end WFM capabilities across forecasting, scheduling, and real-time management.

**Forecasting & Planning**
• Historical data modeling and machine learning forecasting  
• Volume, AHT, and shrinkage prediction by queue and interval  

**Scheduling**
• Automated scheduling and shift optimization  
• Skill-based routing and multi-skill utilization  

**Intraday Management**
• 15-minute interval monitoring  
• Real-time adherence tracking and dynamic adjustments  

**Tools**
• Aspect WFM  
• Real-time dashboards and adherence tools  

This approach ensures SLA adherence, optimized staffing, and cost efficiency.
Confirm ability to deliver Client-provided training modules and track completion (including refresher training).

Ubiquity delivers client-provided training and tracks completion through structured certification frameworks.

• LMS-based tracking (Workday)  
• End-of-training certification (written + practical)  
• Ongoing refresher training based on QA trends  
• Compliance and regulatory training with annual recertification  

Training completion and performance readiness are validated before production deployment.
Describe your approach to nesting/soft-landing and ramp-to-proficiency. Include typical duration.

Ubiquity uses a structured ramp model to ensure smooth transition to production.

• Initial Training: ~3–4 weeks  
• Nesting: ~2 weeks (reduced volume, high coaching support)  

During nesting:
• Real-time coaching and QA monitoring  
• Daily performance reviews  
• Gradual increase in complexity  

Agents must meet certification thresholds (80–85%+) before full production readiness.
Describe how you propose and enforce training graduation criteria (quality/productivity) and how remediation is handled.

Graduation is based on defined quality and productivity thresholds:

**Criteria**
• Certification exams (written + call/chat evaluation)  
• KPI attainment during nesting  

**Enforcement**
• QA monitoring (100% for new hires, then risk-based sampling)  
• Weekly coaching sessions  

**Remediation**
• Targeted retraining and coaching  
• Performance Improvement Plans (PIPs) if needed  

This ensures only fully qualified agents enter production.
Describe your knowledge management approach (job aids, SOP updates, version control, agent communications).

Ubiquity maintains a centralized knowledge management framework:

• Confluence-based knowledge base (SOPs, job aids, FAQs)  
• Version-controlled documentation with regular updates  
• CRM-integrated scripts and decision trees  

**Communication**
• Updates via huddles, email alerts, and training sessions  

**Validation**
• UAT and sandbox testing prior to rollout  

This ensures accuracy, consistency, and rapid adoption of updates.
Describe your QA program, including calibration, sampling methodology, scorecards, and coaching cadence.

Ubiquity’s QA program ensures consistent service quality and compliance.

**Framework**
• 2 audits per agent per week (100% for new hires)  
• Customizable scorecards (CX, Compliance, Hybrid)  

**Calibration**
• Regular internal and client calibration sessions  

**Coaching**
• Weekly 1:1 coaching sessions  
• Real-time feedback for critical errors  

**Reporting**
• Trend analysis, dashboards, and root cause insights  

QA is tightly integrated with training and operations for continuous improvement.
Confirm ability to meet the performance metrics provided in the RFP scope document and identify any metrics you recommend adjusting (with rationale).

Ubiquity can meet or exceed standard performance targets, including:

• Quality: 95%  
• CSAT: 90%  
• First Contact Resolution: 85%  
• Compliance Accuracy: 100%  

All SLAs and KPIs are finalized during contracting and aligned to client requirements.

Ubiquity may recommend adjustments based on program complexity and volume patterns during implementation.
Describe how operational KPIs are monitored (real-time dashboards, daily/weekly reporting) and how corrective actions are managed.

Operational performance is monitored through real-time dashboards and structured reporting.

**Monitoring**
• Real-time dashboards (agent to enterprise level)  
• Daily, weekly, and monthly reporting  

**Corrective Actions**
• Tiered escalation (Supervisor → Manager → Executive)  
• Root Cause Analysis within 24–72 hours  
• Corrective Action Plans with tracked outcomes  

Continuous improvement is driven through QA insights and WFM trends.
Describe your escalation management process for customer complaints and operational risk issues.

Ubiquity uses a structured escalation framework:

• Tier 1: Immediate supervisor response  
• Tier 2: Program leadership with RCA and CAP  
• Tier 3: Executive escalation for critical issues  

An escalation matrix defines triggers, contacts, and SLAs.

Customer complaints and operational risks are tracked, analyzed, and used to drive process improvements.
Describe your experience operating within client-provided CRM/CMS, dialer, ACD/IVR, and call recording environments. Provide examples.

Ubiquity operates within client-provided systems across CRM, telephony, and case management platforms.

**Platforms Supported**
• Salesforce, Zendesk, Kustomer, Intercom  
• Genesys, Amazon Connect, Talkdesk, Twilio  

**Capabilities**
• CRM integration and workflow optimization  
• IVR and ACD configuration  
• Call recording and secure storage  

Ubiquity primarily works within client-owned environments with no local data storage.
Describe your endpoint, network, and access control standards for agent workstations (including remote work, if applicable).

Ubiquity enforces enterprise-grade endpoint and network security controls:

**Endpoints**
• Encrypted devices (AES-256)  
• Endpoint protection and monitoring  

**Network**
• Firewalls, IDS/IPS, and network segmentation  
• TLS 1.2/1.3 encryption for data in transit  

**Access Control**
• Role-based access (RBAC)  
• Multi-factor authentication (MFA)  
• Centralized logging and SIEM monitoring  

Remote work environments follow the same security standards via secure virtual desktops.
Provide your information security certifications/assessments (e.g., SOC reports, ISO certifications) if available, and describe your security governance.

Ubiquity maintains industry-recognized certifications:

• SOC 2 Type II  
• PCI DSS Level 1  
• ISO/IEC 27001  
• HITRUST CSF  

Security governance is led by the Chief Risk & Compliance Officer and aligned with NIST and ISO frameworks.
Describe your incident response process, including notification timelines and breach handling.

Ubiquity maintains a formal Incident Response Plan (IRP):

**Capabilities**
• 24/7 monitoring via SOC and MDR  
• Defined response playbooks  

**Notification**
• Clients notified within 15–30 minutes of confirmed major incidents  

**Post-Incident**
• Root cause analysis and remediation reporting  

Incident response processes are tested annually.
Confirm ability to support Client audit requests (operational and security) and describe your audit support process.

Ubiquity fully supports client operational and security audits.

• Audit logs and activity tracking available  
• Quarterly internal audits and annual external audits  
• Dedicated support for audit requests and evidence submission  

Findings are tracked and remediated through structured action plans.
Describe your compliance program for regulated customer interactions (collections and servicing), including monitoring and corrective action.

Ubiquity maintains a structured compliance program aligned with regulatory requirements.

**Coverage**
• FDCPA, FCRA, GLBA, Reg E, Reg Z, SCRA  

**Controls**
• Script adherence monitoring  
• QA audits and compliance scorecards  

**Training**
• Mandatory onboarding and annual recertification  

Non-compliance triggers immediate remediation and corrective action plans.
Confirm ability to comply with Client policies/SOPs and to promptly notify the Client if potential noncompliance is identified.

Ubiquity adheres to client-managed dialing controls and DNC requirements.

• Operates within client dialer configurations  
• Ensures compliance with TCPA and applicable regulations  
• QA monitoring for adherence  

All dialing activities align with client policies and regulatory standards.
Describe how you manage Do Not Call (DNC) compliance and dialing controls in client-managed dialer environments.

Ubiquity manages Do Not Call (DNC) compliance within client-managed dialer environments by strictly adhering to client configurations, regulatory requirements, and internal control processes.

**Dialer & List Controls**
• Operates exclusively within client-owned dialers (no local list storage)  
• Honors client-configured suppression lists (DNC, internal opt-outs, litigated accounts)  
• Supports real-time and batch list updates from client systems  

**Regulatory Compliance**
• Alignment with TCPA, FDCPA, and applicable state/federal regulations  
• Time-of-day and frequency controls enforced via dialer rules  
• Consent and opt-out status respected across all channels  

**Agent Controls & Training**
• Script adherence and required disclosures embedded in workflows  
• Mandatory compliance training and periodic recertification  
• QA monitoring for dialing behavior and disclosure compliance  

**Monitoring & Auditability**
• Call recordings and dialer logs maintained for audit  
• Regular QA audits and compliance reporting  
• Exception tracking with RCA and corrective actions  

Ubiquity does not override client dialer controls and ensures all activity remains fully auditable and compliant.
Describe your approach to handling SCRA, Bankruptcy, and Credit Bureau Reporting-related back-office work (if in scope).
Provide your standard reporting package outline (daily/weekly/monthly) for voice/chat/back office operations.

Ubiquity provides comprehensive reporting across all channels:

**Daily**
• Operational dashboards and attendance  

**Weekly**
• Performance summaries and QA insights  

**Monthly**
• Business review reports with KPI analysis and RCA  

Reports are customizable and can integrate with client BI systems.
Describe your approach to monthly joint business reviews (JBRs): agenda, attendees, inputs/outputs, and action tracking.

Monthly JBRs include:

**Agenda**
• KPI/SLA performance review  
• Root cause analysis and action plans  

**Attendees**
• Operations, QA, WFM, and client stakeholders  

**Outputs**
• Documented actions and performance improvements  

Progress is tracked through weekly follow-ups.
Describe your invoice validation approach (productive hours, training hours, overtime) and evidence provided to the Client.

Ubiquity bills based on productive hours:

• Includes active work time, training, and coaching  
• Excludes breaks, leave, and non-productive time  

Full transparency is provided through detailed reporting and audit logs.
Describe your business continuity and disaster recovery (BCP/DR) approach for contact center operations, including redundancy and failover between sites.

Ubiquity maintains a robust BCP/DR framework:

• Multi-site redundancy and failover  
• Quarterly testing and validation  
• Backup data centers and secure backups  

Clients are notified within 30 minutes of disruptions, with ongoing updates until resolution.
Provide historical examples of disruption response (e.g., weather events, telecom outages) and outcomes.

Ubiquity has successfully managed multiple disruption scenarios through its Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery (BCP/DR) framework, ensuring minimal client impact and sustained operations.

**1) Severe Weather Event (Philippines – Typhoon Impact)**
• Scenario: Regional typhoon affecting primary delivery sites, impacting transportation and power availability  
• Response:
  – Activated BCP and site-level contingency plans  
  – Shifted workloads to alternate Philippine and nearshore sites  
  – Deployed work-from-home (WFH) capability for critical staff  
  – Utilized backup power and redundant connectivity  
• Outcome:
  – Maintained >95% service level adherence  
  – No material SLA breaches  
  – Full operational normalization within 24–48 hours  

**2) Telecommunications Outage (ISP Disruption)**
• Scenario: Primary ISP outage impacting connectivity at a delivery center  
• Response:
  – Automatic failover to secondary ISP providers  
  – Traffic rerouted to alternate sites where needed  
  – Real-time communication with client stakeholders  
• Outcome:
  – Minimal service interruption (<30 minutes impact)  
  – No data loss or security incidents  
  – SLA performance maintained within acceptable thresholds  

**3) COVID-19 Pandemic (Global Disruption)**
• Scenario: Large-scale disruption requiring rapid shift to remote operations  
• Response:
  – Rapid deployment of secure WFH environments (VDI/VPN-enabled)  
  – Scaled remote workforce while maintaining PCI-compliant controls  
  – Adjusted staffing models and cross-trained teams  
• Outcome:
  – Transitioned majority of workforce to remote within weeks  
  – Maintained continuous operations across all programs  
  – Sustained SLA and quality performance throughout transition  

**4) Power Outage / Facility-Level Disruption**
• Scenario: Localized power interruption at a delivery site  
• Response:
  – Immediate failover to backup generators and UPS systems  
  – Partial workload redistribution to other sites  
• Outcome:
  – No significant downtime for critical functions  
  – Seamless continuation of operations  

**Key Strengths Across All Scenarios**
• Multi-site, globally distributed delivery model  
• Redundant infrastructure (power, network, systems)  
• Real-time monitoring and rapid escalation protocols  
• Tested BCP/DR plans with defined RTO (24–72 hours)  

These examples demonstrate Ubiquity’s ability to maintain operational continuity, protect client data, and meet service commitments during disruptive events.
Confirm ability to provide multi-site delivery and describe how work would be redistributed during an outage.

Ubiquity supports multi-site delivery with dynamic workload redistribution.

• Real-time traffic rerouting  
• Cross-site staffing flexibility  
• Automatic ISP failover  

Ensures continuity during outages or disruptions.
Provide a proposed transition plan and timeline for go-live, including recruiting, training, technology readiness, and cutover.

Ubiquity delivers transitions through a structured PMO-led implementation framework, ensuring governance, transparency, and on-time delivery.

**Overall Timeline:** ~8–12 weeks (subject to scope)

**Governance**
• Dedicated Implementation Manager (PMO)  
• Workstream leads (Recruiting, Training, WFM, IT, Compliance)  
• Weekly status reporting, RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies)  
• Stage-gate approvals  

**Phased Approach**

1) **Initiation & Discovery (Weeks 1–2)**
• Kickoff and PMO governance setup  
• Requirements gathering across all workstreams  
• Process mapping, KPI/SLA alignment, risk assessment  

2) **Design & Planning (Weeks 2–4)**
• Detailed project plan and resource model  
• Technology design (connectivity, access, security)  
• Training curriculum and hiring plan finalized  

3) **Build (Weeks 4–8)**
• Recruiting and hiring (4–6 weeks typical)  
• System configuration and access provisioning  
• Training delivery (3–4 weeks)  

4) **Test & Readiness (Weeks 8–10)**
• User Acceptance Testing (UAT)  
• Certification of agents (80–85%+ thresholds)  
• Operational readiness validation  

5) **Cutover & Go-Live (Weeks 10–12)**
• Go/No-Go decision (PMO + client sign-off)  
• Controlled ramp and live production launch  

6) **Stabilization (Post Go-Live)**
• Hypercare support (2–4 weeks)  
• Daily performance monitoring and issue resolution  
• Transition to steady-state operations  

This PMO-led approach ensures controlled execution, clear accountability, and minimized delivery risk.
Confirm ability to complete transition within ~180 calendar days (subject to final scope) and identify key dependencies.

Ubiquity confirms the ability to complete transition within ~180 calendar days and, in most cases, delivers within 8–12 weeks depending on scope and complexity.

**Key Dependencies**
• Final scope alignment and timely approvals  
• Client availability for design workshops and sign-offs  
• System access and connectivity readiness  
• Defined hiring profiles and volume forecasts  
• Timely completion of UAT and Go/No-Go approvals  

The PMO manages all dependencies through a centralized RAID log, with proactive mitigation and escalation to ensure timeline adherence.
Describe your approach to knowledge transfer, documentation, and operational readiness sign-off.

Ubiquity follows a structured knowledge transfer and readiness framework governed by PMO controls and defined stage gates.

**Knowledge Transfer**
• Train-the-Trainer (T3) model for scalable knowledge distribution  
• Blended delivery (instructor-led, eLearning, simulations)  
• Shadowing, reverse shadowing, and certification assessments  

**Documentation**
• SOPs, job aids, and process maps maintained in a centralized knowledge base  
• Version control and approval workflows  
• Documented data flows, escalation paths, and compliance requirements  

**Operational Readiness (Stage-Gate Sign-Off)**
Readiness is validated through formal PMO checkpoints:

• Training Certification (agents meet 80–85% thresholds)  
• System Readiness (access, tools, security validated)  
• UAT Completion (client-approved)  
• Staffing Readiness (hiring and scheduling aligned)  

Final readiness is confirmed through a **Go/No-Go decision framework**, requiring joint sign-off from Ubiquity PMO and client stakeholders prior to production launch.
Describe your approach to exit management and orderly transition support at contract end or termination.

Ubiquity supports structured exit management and transition services to ensure continuity and minimal disruption at contract end or termination.

**Exit Governance**
• Transition plan with defined timeline and milestones  
• Regular status reporting and risk tracking  

**Knowledge Transfer**
• Documentation handover (SOPs, process maps, training materials)  
• Train-the-trainer support for incoming vendor or client teams  
• Access to historical reporting and performance data  

**Operational Transition**
• Phased ramp-down aligned to client timelines  
• Parallel operations (if required) to ensure continuity  
• Controlled knowledge migration and system access transition  

**Data Management**
• Secure data return or destruction per client requirements  
• Certification of data deletion aligned with regulatory standards  

Ubiquity remains fully engaged through transition completion to ensure service continuity, compliance, and a seamless handover.
Confirm ability to submit pricing in the provided quote template and describe any alternative commercial models you propose.

Ubiquity confirms the ability to submit pricing using the client-provided quote template.

Pricing will be finalized based on confirmed scope, volumes, service levels, and delivery model during the discovery phase.

**Standard Commercial Model**
• Per productive hour (fully loaded rate)

**Alternative Models (available upon request)**
• FTE-based monthly pricing  
• Transaction-based pricing (per case / per interaction)  
• Outcome-based or hybrid models (aligned to KPIs/SLA performance)  

Ubiquity is flexible in structuring commercial models to align with client objectives, including cost predictability and performance alignment.
Describe your billing methodology (productive hour/minute definition, rounding/increments, treatment of shrinkage, etc.).

Ubiquity bills based on **productive hours**, defined as time during which an agent is logged in, available, and performing work on the client program.

**Included (Billable Time)**
• Live production (calls, chats, back-office work)  
• Training (new hire and refresher)  
• Coaching and required meetings  

**Excluded (Non-Billable Time)**
• Breaks, meals, and idle time  
• Vacation, holidays, and sick leave  

**Billing Structure**
• Hourly billing (no fractional minute billing unless specified)  
• Standard monthly FTE assumption: ~145 productive hours  

**Shrinkage**
• Ubiquity absorbs shrinkage (e.g., breaks, absenteeism), ensuring predictable billing  

Detailed reporting and audit logs are provided to support invoice validation and transparency.
Describe proposed annual escalation methodology and any volume-based pricing tiers.

**Annual Escalation**
Ubiquity applies standard annual rate adjustments aligned to:
• Labor market conditions  
• Inflation indices (where applicable)  
• Geographic cost changes  

Escalation terms are defined contractually and typically reviewed annually.

**Volume-Based Pricing**
Ubiquity supports tiered pricing models based on scale:

• Lower unit rates at higher volume tiers  
• Blended rates for multi-channel or multi-skill programs  
• Flexibility for ramp-up/ramp-down scenarios  

Pricing is structured to incentivize scale efficiency while maintaining service quality.
Identify any one-time implementation fees, pass-through costs, or minimum commitments assumed.

**One-Time Fees**
• Implementation / setup fee (covers onboarding, training, IT setup, and program launch)

**Pass-Through Costs (if applicable)**
• Telephony (usage-based, if Ubiquity platform is used)  
• Professional services (e.g., consulting, integrations, IVR design)  

**Minimum Commitment**
• Typical minimum: 10 FTEs (subject to agreement)  

All costs are transparently defined during contracting.
Describe any proposed performance credits/penalties or gainshare structures tied to SLA achievement.

Ubiquity supports performance-based commercial structures aligned to SLA achievement.

**Standard Approach**
• Service credits or penalties tied to SLA/KPI performance  
• Metrics may include quality, SLA adherence, productivity, and compliance  

**Optional Gainshare Models**
• Incentives tied to exceeding targets (e.g., improved collections, efficiency gains, CSAT improvements)  

All performance mechanisms are mutually defined during contracting to ensure alignment with business outcomes.

Describe your subcontracting policy and any anticipated subcontractors for this engagement.

Ubiquity does not utilize subcontractors or fourth-party vendors to deliver core services for this engagement.

If third-party vendors are required (e.g., technology providers), they are:
• Pre-approved  
• Contractually bound to Ubiquity security and compliance standards  
• Aligned with data protection and confidentiality requirements  

No subcontractors are currently anticipated for this engagement.
Confirm ability to comply with data confidentiality requirements and describe data retention/return processes.

Ubiquity maintains a defense-in-depth approach to data confidentiality, aligned with regulatory and client requirements.

**Data Protection**
• Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2/1.3)  
• Role-based access control (RBAC) and least privilege  
• Continuous monitoring and audit logging  

**Data Retention**
• Retention aligned to client requirements and regulatory standards  
• Examples:
  – Reg E: minimum 2 years  
  – Reg Z: minimum 3 years  
• Default retention:
  – Call recordings: 90 days  
  – Sensitive data: up to 1 year (extendable per contract, up to 7 years)

**Data Return & Destruction**
• Secure transfer via SFTP or cloud storage (e.g., AWS S3)  
• Certified data destruction using NIST SP 800-88 compliant methods  
• Media sanitization and secure disposal  

All data handling processes are auditable and compliant with applicable regulations.

Ubiquity provides dedicated fraud operations teams composed of analysts, QA specialists, team leads, and program managers.

• Fraud Analysts (Level 1–3)  
• QA Analysts  
• Team Leads / Supervisors  
• Operations Managers  

**Tenure**
• Average agent tenure: 1.5–3+ years (program-dependent)  
• Leadership tenure: 3–7+ years  

Teams are structured to support both intake and investigation functions with clear separation of duties.

Ubiquity has supported banking and financial services clients since its founding in 2012, including fraud operations, dispute management, and risk & compliance services.

This includes over a decade of experience supporting fraud-related processes across card and lending products.

Operations are aligned with U.S. regulatory requirements (Reg E, Reg Z, NACHA, card network rules) and client-specific policies.

Ubiquity does not deploy its own fraud detection engine but provides trained analysts and process expertise to operate and optimize client-owned platforms.
[Fraud RFI 4.2] Describe experience working directly in client-owned systems.

Ubiquity agents work directly within client-owned systems with no local data storage.

All activities are performed within secure, access-controlled environments with full audit logging.

• Access granted via RBAC and least privilege

[Fraud RFI 4.5] Describe audit logging, monitoring, and evidence retention for system activity.

Ubiquity uses AI and automation to enhance fraud operations:

All automation operates within client-defined controls.
[Fraud RFI 5.1] Describe your information security governance framework and certifications (SOC 1/2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS).

Ubiquity maintains a comprehensive Information Security Governance framework aligned with industry best practices and regulatory standards.

**Governance Framework**
• Led by the Chief Risk & Compliance Officer (CRCO) with oversight from a dedicated Information Security and Compliance team  
• Policies and controls aligned to NIST, ISO/IEC 27001, and CIS Benchmarks  
• Enterprise-wide governance supported by formal risk management, internal audit, and policy management programs  
• Policies are reviewed at least annually and updated based on regulatory, threat, or business changes  

**Certifications & Independent Assessments**
• SOC 2 Type II: Independently audited annually, covering security, availability, and confidentiality controls  
• ISO/IEC 27001: Certified Information Security Management System (ISMS) across enterprise operations  
• PCI DSS Level 1: Certified across delivery sites, including on-site and remote environments, with annual QSA audits  

**Additional Compliance & Framework Alignment**
• HITRUST CSF Certified  
• Compliance with GDPR, HIPAA, and CCPA (as applicable)  

Ubiquity’s governance model ensures continuous monitoring, auditability, and alignment with client and regulatory security requirements.
[Fraud RFI 5.2] Describe controls for protection of customer PII and sensitive banking data.

Ubiquity applies a defense-in-depth security model to protect customer PII and sensitive banking data, combining technical, administrative, and physical controls aligned with industry standards.

**Access Control & Monitoring**
• Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and least-privilege enforcement  
• Segregation of Duties (SoD) to reduce insider risk  
• Centralized logging and continuous monitoring via Security Operations Center (SOC)  
• Regular user access reviews and audit trails  

**Encryption & Data Protection**
• Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2/1.3)  
• Secure data transfer protocols (SFTP, HTTPS)  
• Tokenization, truncation, and masking of sensitive data (e.g., PAN)  
• Strong key management aligned with NIST and ISO standards  
• Prohibition of transmitting sensitive data via unsecured channels  

**Network & System Security**
• Network segmentation and environment isolation  
• Firewalls, IDS/IPS, and proxy-based URL whitelisting  
• Endpoint hardening aligned to CIS benchmarks  
• Regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing  

**Physical Security Controls**
• Restricted access to production areas (ID badge + controlled entry)  
• 24/7 security, CCTV monitoring, and escorted visitor access  
• Clear desk/screen policy and secure equipment handling  

**Data Resilience & Governance**
• Secure backups and disaster recovery controls  
• Formal security policies with annual review and updates  
• Ongoing employee security training and awareness programs  

**Compliance & Assurance**
• Aligned with PCI DSS Level 1, SOC 2 Type II, ISO/IEC 27001, and HITRUST  
• Compliant with GDPR, HIPAA, and CCPA (as applicable)  

These layered controls ensure confidentiality, integrity, and availability of sensitive customer data across all operations.
[Fraud RFI 5.3] Explain how data access is restricted, monitored, and logged for fraud operations staff.

Ubiquity enforces strict controls to ensure data access is appropriately restricted, continuously monitored, and fully auditable for fraud operations staff.

**Access Restriction**
• Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) aligned to job responsibilities  
• Least-privilege and “need-to-know” access enforcement  
• Client-specific access segregation to prevent cross-client data exposure  
• Access provisioning and deprovisioning through formal approval workflows  

**Monitoring**
• Continuous monitoring via centralized Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) platforms  
• Real-time alerting for anomalous or unauthorized access activity  
• Regular user access reviews to validate ongoing authorization  

**Logging & Auditability**
• All access and user activities are logged (user ID, timestamp, action performed)  
• Logs are securely stored and retained for audit and forensic analysis  
• Audit trails are available to support internal reviews and client audit requests  

These controls ensure full traceability, minimize unauthorized access risk, and support regulatory and audit requirements.
[Fraud RFI 5.4] Are cell phones or any data recording (verbal, video, picture) devices of employees restricted in use during working hours or in sensitve work areas?

Yes. Personal devices (cell phones, cameras, recording devices) are strictly prohibited in production areas.

Controls include:
• Secured production floors  
• Device-free zones  
• Monitoring and enforcement policies
[Fraud RFI 5.5] Describe endpoint, network, and remote access security controls for offshore resources.

Ubiquity enforces enterprise-grade endpoint, network, and remote access security controls across offshore delivery environments.

**Endpoint Security**
• Hardened workstations aligned to CIS benchmarks  
• Full disk encryption (AES-256)  
• Endpoint protection (anti-malware, device control, monitoring tools)  
• USB and external media restrictions (as required)  

**Network Security**
• Segmented network architecture separating production and non-production environments  
• Firewalls with strict ingress/egress filtering  
• Intrusion Detection/Prevention Systems (IDS/IPS)  
• Proxy-based internet access with URL whitelisting  

**Remote Access Security**
• Secure access via VPN or virtual desktop environments (e.g., VDI/AWS Workspaces)  
• Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all remote and privileged access  
• Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with least-privilege enforcement  
• Continuous monitoring via SIEM and Security Operations Center (SOC)  

These controls ensure secure access to client systems while maintaining data protection and regulatory compliance.
[Fraud RFI 5.6] Describe physical security controls at delivery locations.

Ubiquity maintains robust physical security controls across all delivery locations to protect personnel, infrastructure, and sensitive data.

**Access Controls**
• Badge-based access with electronic access control systems  
• Restricted entry to production areas and critical zones (e.g., server rooms)  
• Multi-factor authentication for high-security areas (e.g., data centers)  

**Monitoring & Surveillance**
• 24/7 on-site security personnel  
• CCTV monitoring of entry points and operational areas  

**Visitor Management**
• Visitor registration, ID verification, and badge issuance  
• Mandatory escort within secure areas  

**Operational Controls**
• Client-level physical segregation where required  
• Clear desk and clear screen policies  
• Prohibition of unauthorized device usage and asset removal  

**Facility Resilience**
• Fire detection and suppression systems  
• Backup power and environmental controls  

These measures ensure controlled access, continuous monitoring, and protection of client operations.
[Fraud RFI 5.7] Describe incident response, breach notification, and regulatory escalation procedures.

Ubiquity maintains a formal Incident Response Plan (IRP) to detect, contain, and remediate security incidents in a timely and controlled manner.

**Detection & Monitoring**
• 24/7 monitoring via Security Operations Center (SOC) and Managed Detection & Response (MDR)  
• Real-time alerting and anomaly detection  

**Response Framework**
• Structured playbooks covering identification, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident review  
• Clearly defined roles, escalation paths, and communication protocols  

**Breach Notification**
• Client notification within 15–30 minutes of confirmed major incidents  
• Ongoing updates provided through agreed communication channels  

**Regulatory & Escalation Procedures**
• Alignment with regulatory requirements for incident reporting  
• Escalation to executive leadership and compliance teams as required  

**Post-Incident Management**
• Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and Corrective Action Plans (CAPs)  
• Formal incident reports and lessons learned  

Incident response processes are tested regularly through tabletop and simulation exercises.
[Fraud RFI 5.8] Provide your typical securty and architectural diagram for both system access, security, and client connectivity. What are the encryption methods in place?

Ubiquity’s security architecture is designed to ensure secure system access, client connectivity, and data protection across all environments.

**Architecture Overview**
• Secure client connectivity via VPN, IPsec tunnels, or private network links  
• Integration through APIs (REST/SOAP) and secure file transfer (SFTP, HTTPS, AWS S3)  
• Network segmentation to isolate client environments and minimize risk exposure  
• Access governed by RBAC, least privilege, and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)  
• Centralized logging and monitoring via SIEM and SOC  

**Encryption Standards**
• Data at rest: AES-256 encryption  
• Data in transit: TLS 1.2/1.3, HTTPS, SFTP, IPsec VPN  
• Key management aligned with NIST and ISO standards  

**Additional Controls**
• Secure authentication and session management  
• Audit logging for all system and user activities  
• Regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing  

This architecture ensures confidentiality, integrity, and secure connectivity for all client data and systems.
[Fraud RFI 5.9] What is the disaster recovery and business continuity solution in place?  What is the recovery time (RTO)?

Ubiquity maintains a comprehensive Business Continuity Plan (BCP) and Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) to ensure service continuity and rapid recovery.

**Capabilities**
• Multi-site delivery with failover capability  
• Redundant infrastructure and data backups (offsite/cloud)  
• Crisis Management Team (BCMT) and command center activation  
• Quarterly testing (failover and simulation exercises)  

**Recovery Objectives**
• RTO: Typically within 24–72 hours (program-specific)  
• RPO: Defined per client and data criticality  

Plans are reviewed and tested regularly to ensure operational resilience and minimal disruption.
[Fraud RFI 5.10] How is client data isolated and segregrated between clients?

Ubiquity enforces strict logical and physical data segregation across clients.

**Logical Controls**
• Network segmentation and environment isolation  
• Client-specific RBAC permissions  
• Dedicated system access and data partitioning  

**Physical Controls**
• Segregated work areas (as required by client)  
• Restricted access via badge controls  

These controls prevent cross-client data exposure and ensure secure multi-tenant operations.
[Fraud RFI 5.11] How is AI leveaged in your outsourcing processes?  What services are used/available?

Ubiquity leverages AI and automation to enhance fraud operations while maintaining human oversight.

**Use Cases**
• AI-driven QA and speech analytics (e.g., ZViQ)  
• Predictive analytics and trend detection  
• RPA (UiPath) for repetitive workflows (e.g., case handling)  
• Real-time coaching and performance insights (Aigent)  

**Capabilities**
• Workflow automation and case prioritization  
• Sentiment and behavioral analysis  
• Integration with client systems and data sources  

All AI usage operates within client-defined controls and data security requirements.

Access is only granted upon full compliance with security requirements.
[Fraud RFI 6.3] Explain how conflicts of interest are identified and managed.

Ubiquity manages conflicts of interest through:

• Policies prohibiting unauthorized data access or misuse  
• Segregation of duties and role-based controls  

Potential conflicts are escalated to Compliance and mitigated through reassignment or access restriction.
[Fraud RFI 6.4] Describe ongoing monitoring or re-screening practices for employees.

• Security awareness programs (e.g., phishing simulations)  

These controls ensure sustained compliance and risk mitigation.

[Fraud RFI 7.3] List professional certifications held by fraud staff (CFE, CAMS, CFCS).

Client-specific policies and risk appetite are embedded through:

Ensures alignment with client risk tolerance and regulatory expectations.
[Fraud RFI 7.5] Provide average analyst tenure and annual attrition rates.

• Annual attrition: ~5–6% (recent trends)  

Retention is supported through career development and engagement programs.
[Fraud RFI 8.1] Describe experience across channels (card, ACH, wire, ATO, P2P).

Ubiquity has extensive experience supporting fraud operations across multiple payment channels for banking and fintech clients.

**Channels Supported**
• Card (debit and credit): fraud intake, dispute handling, chargebacks, and transaction investigations  
• ACH: unauthorized transaction reviews, return processing, and Reg E compliance  
• Wire: high-risk transaction validation, escalation handling, and investigation support  
• Account Takeover (ATO): identity verification, account recovery, and fraud mitigation  
• P2P / Digital Payments: unauthorized transfer investigations and dispute resolution  

**Experience & Scale**
• ~1.5M+ disputed transactions processed monthly  
• Support across end-to-end workflows including intake, investigation, disposition, and reporting  

Ubiquity operates within client-defined systems, policies, and regulatory frameworks to ensure consistent, compliant fraud handling across all channels.
[Fraud RFI 8.2] Describe decision-making approach in ambiguous fraud scenarios.

Ubiquity applies a structured, risk-based decision framework for ambiguous fraud scenarios.

• Decisions guided by client-defined rules, thresholds, and regulatory requirements  
• Analysis of transaction data, behavioral patterns, and historical activity  
• Review of supporting evidence (e.g., device data, prior disputes, customer statements)  
• Escalation to senior analysts or supervisors for complex or unclear cases  

Where required, dual-review protocols are applied to ensure accuracy and consistency.
[Fraud RFI 8.3] Explain how analytics, rules, or AI support analyst decisions.

Analyst decisions are supported through a combination of rules, analytics, and AI:

• Client-defined rules engines and decision frameworks  
• Real-time dashboards and historical reporting for trend visibility  
• AI-driven analytics (e.g., QA, sentiment, anomaly detection)  
• RPA for workflow automation and case handling efficiency  

These tools enhance decision accuracy while maintaining human oversight.
[Fraud RFI 8.4] Describe how emerging fraud trends are identified and escalated.

Ubiquity identifies and escalates emerging fraud trends through:

• Analysis of internal case data and QA findings  
• Monitoring of industry alerts, card network updates, and client inputs  
• Trend reporting and escalation to client stakeholders  

Validated trends are incorporated into SOPs, training, and rule updates to mitigate risk.
[Fraud RFI 8.5] Describe end-to-end fraud alert review and disposition process.

Ubiquity supports the full fraud lifecycle:

1. Intake & Triage (alerts, customer contact, or system triggers)  
2. Case Creation & Classification  
3. Investigation (transaction review, behavioral analysis, documentation)  
4. Decision & Disposition (fraud / non-fraud / escalate)  
5. Action (e.g., blocking, chargeback, recovery)  
6. Documentation & Reporting  

All activities are tracked within case management systems with full auditability.
[Fraud RFI 8.6] Explain controls governing account blocking and unblocking actions.

Account blocking/unblocking actions are governed by strict controls:

• Executed per client SOPs and authorization rules  
• Role-based access restrictions (RBAC)  
• Mandatory logging of all actions  

Controls ensure actions are consistent, authorized, and auditable.
[Fraud RFI 8.7] Describe dual-control or approval requirements for high-risk actions.

High-risk actions (e.g., account unblocking) require dual control:

• Secondary approval by supervisor or authorized personnel  
• Segregation of duties enforced  

Applied based on client-defined risk thresholds.
[Fraud RFI 8.8] Describe how improper blocks/unblocks are detected and remediated.

Improper blocks/unblocks are identified through:

• QA audits and monitoring  
• Exception and variance reporting  
• Customer complaints and escalations  

Remediation includes RCA, corrective actions, retraining, and process updates.
[Fraud RFI 8.9] Describe documentation and audit trails maintained for fraud decisions.

Ubiquity maintains comprehensive documentation and audit trails:

• Full case history and supporting evidence  
• System logs (user ID, timestamp, actions taken)  
• Decision rationale and outcomes  

All records are retained for audit, compliance, and forensic review.
[Fraud RFI 8.10] Describe your overall SLA framework and governance for fraud operations engagements.

Ubiquity defines SLAs jointly with clients, supported by structured governance.

**Framework**
• Defined SLAs for response, resolution, quality, and compliance  
• Real-time monitoring and reporting  

**Governance**
• Weekly/monthly reviews  
• RCA (24–72 hours) for SLA misses  
• CAP tracking and continuous improvement  

SLAs are aligned to client requirements and regulatory expectations.
[Fraud RFI 8.11] Describe your typical Fraud Alert Handling & Timeliness SLAs .

Typical SLAs include:

• Alert review: near real-time to same-day (based on priority)  
• Case resolution: ~5 calendar days (standard benchmark)  

SLA targets are finalized per client requirements.
[Fraud RFI 8.12] Describe your typical Account blocking/unblocking SLAs.

• Blocking: near real-time or within minutes for high-risk events  
• Unblocking: same-day or within defined SLA after validation  

Aligned to risk level and client policies.
[Fraud RFI 8.13] Describe your escalation and handoff SLAs.

• Tier 1 acknowledgement: within 1–2 hours  
• RCA completion: within 24–72 hours  
• CAP implementation: within ~14 days  

Escalations follow a structured tiered model.
[Fraud RFI 8.14] What are your quality and accuracy SLAs.

Typical targets:

• Quality: ≥95%  
• Compliance accuracy: 100%  
• CSAT: ~90%  
• First Contact Resolution: ~85%  

Final targets are agreed during contracting.
[Fraud RFI 8.15] Are service credits tied to SLA falures?  If so, describe the process.

Yes. Ubiquity supports service credits tied to SLA performance.

• Credits triggered by missed SLAs  
• RCA and CAP required for all breaches  
• Optional gainshare for exceeding targets  

Terms are defined contractually.
[Fraud RFI 9.1] Describe your experience supporting bank regulatory exams or audits.

Ubiquity supports regulatory and client audits through dedicated audit, risk, and compliance teams.

• Experience with internal, client, and third-party audits  
• Findings tracked in centralized action registry  
• Full audit support including documentation and remediation
[Fraud RFI 9.2] Explain how you support OCC/FDIC third-party risk management expectations.

Ubiquity aligns with third-party risk management expectations:

• Vendor risk management and audit readiness  
• Transparent reporting and governance  

Supports client compliance with OCC/FDIC requirements.
[Fraud RFI 9.3] Describe how regulatory inquiries, MRAs, or issues are supported and remediated.

• Issues logged in centralized risk registry  
• RCA and CAP development with defined ownership  
• Ongoing tracking until closure  

Ensures timely and auditable remediation.
[Fraud RFI 9.4] Describe documentation, evidence, and SME support provided during exams.

• Preparation of documentation and evidence  
• SME participation during audits and reviews  
• Support for RCA, CAP, and reporting  

Ensures readiness and responsiveness during exams.
[Fraud RFI 9.5] Describe how regulatory changes are monitored and operationalized.

Ubiquity operationalizes regulatory changes through:

• Policy updates and version-controlled documentation

• Governance through GRC platform and Policy Committee  

Ensures timely and controlled implementation of regulatory changes.
